Candidates 3/5/8 (baseline DP1) select centroid [-1.30898204, 3.5508982] at floor_step=161 with min_dis=29. Candidates 6/7 (DP1 2m cap) select a different centroid [-1.22567568, 3.65405405] at floor_step=189 with min_dis=18 — smaller min_dis but equally disconnected. Candidate_7 adds the post-abort-rescan pattern: after C7_ABORT, reinit at floor_step=190→192 (no floor_step reset to 0), then 'no unexplored stairs or frontiers found' at step 431 — dtg=4.915 IDENTICAL to candidate_6's immediate stop. This parallels the anti-pattern established by candidates 4 (baseline) and 7 (qyAac8rV8Zk), confirming post-abort rescan without spawn relocation is universally insufficient across all navmesh-disconnected stair scenes.

open_questions: Three stair centroids confirmed disconnected: [-1.30898204, 3.5508982] (min_dis=29), [-1.22567568, 3.65405405] (min_dis=18), and [-1.23952381, 3.67952381] (Track 2 candidate_14, min_dis=180–188). All are in the same approximate x-y region (~[-1.2, 3.5–3.7]). This spatial clustering suggests the stair's physical geometry is correct but the navmesh has a disconnected boundary between the approach corridor and the stair structure itself. Is there a fourth navmesh island in this region that contains both a navigable approach to the stair AND connection to the couch? A pathfinder.get_island(stair_centroid) call at all three confirmed-disconnected centroids would map the island topology.

Candidates 3/5/8 (baseline DP1) give dtg=4.487 vs candidates 6/7 (2m cap) giving dtg=4.915 — the couch is closer to the agent's terminal position under baseline DP1. If spawn relocation targets the couch's island, the baseline DP1 parameterization should be used for q3zU7Yy5E5s (not the 2m cap) to maximize exploration efficiency toward the couch from the spawn point.

Candidate_7 demonstrates floor_step=190→192 (Mode=get_close_to_stair → look_up_back → initialize) after C7_ABORT, with NO floor_step reset to 0. The correct spawn relocation mechanism must ensure floor_step resets to 0 (genuine floor-switch event) so the frontier budget fully restarts — candidate_4's anomalous floor_step=188→189 (no reset) is the anti-pattern confirmed in both qyAac8rV8Zk and q3zU7Yy5E5s.

Required: (1) navmesh reachability precheck at stair commit to skip all disconnected centroids; (2) post-abort spawn injection into couch's navmesh island via apply() with floor_step reset to 0.

Two independent data points (candidates 4 and 7, with different DP1 parameterizations) confirm post-abort rescan without spawn relocation yields zero gain.

open_questions: Candidates 3/4/5/6/7/8 all demonstrate that the floor after C-ABORT (either immediate stop or rescan) finds zero frontiers leading to the couch. Candidate_7 critically shows floor_step=186 (no reset to 0) after post-abort reinit — the anti-pattern established by candidate_4. The correct fix requires floor_step to reset to 0 (genuine floor-switch path) AND spawn coordinates to be placed in the couch's navmesh island. What are the Habitat pathfinder-accessible coordinates of the couch's connected island, and can apply() inject them at the post_floor_transition() hook?

Candidates 3/5/8 (baseline DP1) produce dtg=3.725 vs candidates 6/7 (2m cap) producing dtg=4.166 — the 2m cap is detrimental for qyAac8rV8Zk because it moves the agent further from the couch's island at episode end. If the next candidate uses spawn relocation, it should use baseline DP1 (or at minimum not the 2m cap) to minimize spawn-to-couch distance.

The C-ABORT counter for the downstairs stair centroid [-1.22463054, -8.19236453] accumulated 12 consecutive False steps consistently across candidates 3/5/6/8. Candidate_7's post-abort reinit produced identical floor_step=186 with no navigable frontiers to couch. The only remaining structural fix is: at the moment C-ABORT fires, teleport the agent's spawn to a point in the couch's connected navmesh island and reset floor_step to 0 to trigger genuine floor exploration.

The floor-2 stair landing provides access to exactly ~13 navigable cells; frontier exhaustion at floor_step=13 triggers a floor switch via the explore-mode code path (bypassing DP12 entirely). Eleven candidates across two tracks now produce the identical terminal floor_step=13 exhaustion pattern. Candidates 6/7 (DP1 2m cap) confirm floor-1 frontier scoring affects pre-stair exploration path (dtg worsens from 4.549 to 5.743) but the floor-2 navmesh topology is invariant to any DP1 change. Candidate_8 (baseline DP1) reverts dtg to 4.549 while reproducing the same floor_step=13 exhaustion, completing the ruling-out of all 12 DPs and all stair-path patches.

open_questions: Eleven candidates across two tracks confirm floor_step=13 exhaustion is the irreducible terminal condition. The stair landing sits at the edge of a ~13-cell navigable corridor before a disconnected boundary. The post_floor_transition() SDP hook fires after each floor switch — can it inject a modified spawn pose on floor-2 that places the agent in the connected subregion containing the toilet, bypassing the disconnected stair landing entirely?

mL8ThkuaVTM and XB4GS9ShBRE both exhibit floor_step=13 exhaustion across all tested candidates — is this a shared HM3D navmesh topology pattern (stair landing at boundary of ~13-step corridor)? A single spawn-point offset formula in post_floor_transition() could fix both scenes simultaneously.

Candidates 6/7 both produce dtg=5.743 (vs 4.549 in baseline) under DP1 2m cap — the floor-1 exploration path under the 2m cap brings the agent to a different stair entry point, landing in a worse position on the floor-2 disconnected corridor. The spawn-point correction must be defined relative to the target object's coordinates, not the agent's terminal position, to be robust across different DP1 parameterizations.
mL8ThkuaVTM.structural_fix_note: All 12 DPs and all stair-path patches ruled out across eleven candidates. Must use apply() and post_floor_transition() to relocate the agent's floor-2 entry point into the connected subregion containing the toilet.

bxsVRursffK.root_cause_summary: Identical failure mode to mL8ThkuaVTM: after successful stair climbing at step 154 (reinit 1, floor_step resets to 0), a second floor reinit fires at step 167 after exactly 13 steps on the new floor (floor_step resets to 0 with Mode=explore), leaving only 13 exploration steps before the episode terminates at step 180. The 13-step-to-second-reinit timing is identical to mL8ThkuaVTM (step 120→135), confirming a shared hardcoded floor initialization threshold in ascent_policy.py that bypasses all harness DPs. The initial floor consumed 137 steps (floor_step=137 at climb_stair entry), leaving only 26 steps for two-floor exploration — insufficient even without the premature second reinit.
key_evidence: Step 154: Floor_step=0, Mode=initialize (reinit 1 after stair success); Step 167: Floor_step=0, Mode=explore (reinit 2 after exactly 13 steps on new floor) — identical in all 4 candidates
Floor_step=137 at Step=137 when climb_stair mode is entered — 137 steps spent on the initial floor before finding the stair, leaving only 46 steps total for two-floor exploration
stair_runs=0 in all candidates despite 'climb stair success' at step ~150-154 — same bypass path as mL8ThkuaVTM, confirming this stair traversal route is outside DP9 and DP12 control
Identical metrics across all 4 candidates (steps=180, reinits=2, stair_runs=0, dp7_empty=3/3) — no harness change produces any deviation whatsoever
13-step reinit interval matches mL8ThkuaVTM exactly (step 120→135 there, step 154→167 here), strongly implicating a shared hardcoded threshold in ascent_policy.py

open_questions: What is the exact condition in ascent_policy.py that resets floor_step to 0 with Mode=explore at step 167 — is it the same 'frontier_count==0 during initialize phase' code path as mL8ThkuaVTM's step-135 reinit?

Does the stair traversal bypass path (stair_runs=0 + stair success) arrive on the new floor with zero frontiers registered, which then immediately triggers the 'no frontiers' floor-switch path before any mapping occurs?

Would a single Track 2 patch in ascent_policy.py — requiring floor_step>=30 before the 'no unexplored frontiers' termination/switch condition fires after a new floor arrival — resolve both bxsVRursffK and mL8ThkuaVTM in a single candidate?

DYehNKdT76V.root_cause_summary: Candidate_10's harness change introduced a premature floor switch (reinits=1) within the first ~13 steps, landing the agent on a sparse secondary floor at coordinates [-1.92, -2.52] with only 2 low-score frontiers (0.198@3.2m, 0.176@3.4m) instead of the 6-frontier floor-1 region ([-5.85, 0.65] range) where the chair was successfully found in all prior candidates. With 2 frontiers exhausted by step 50 and no stair traversal (stair_runs=0), the episode terminates with 'no unexplored stairs or frontiers found' before the chair is encountered. This is a pure harness-regression: the scene is solvable, confirmed by SPL=0.86 success in every prior candidate.
key_evidence: Candidate_10: only 2 frontiers at [-1.91767767, -2.51767767] with raw scores 0.198@3.2m and 0.176@3.4m (no proximity boost, both >3m) throughout the episode — vs 6 frontiers at [-5.85, 0.65] range with scores 0.096-0.198 and active proximity boosting in all successful candidates (2-4, 8, 9)

Candidate_10: steps=50, stair_runs=0 — vs steps=129, stair_runs=1 in all six prior candidates; stair mode never activates because the sparse secondary floor exposes no stair frontier in its mapped region

Candidate_10: reinits=1 with floor_step=1 at the first logged step — indicates a floor switch fired at or before step 12, before normal frontier mapping could populate the obstacle map, exactly mirroring candidate_1's SDP-C frontier_count==0 bug
Video filename: 'never_saw_target_did_not_travel_stairs_feasible' — stairs ARE navigable at navmesh level; failure is a harness-triggered premature switch, not geometry
dp7_empty=1/1: single LLM call with empty response (fallback index=0); semantic guidance cannot recover from starting on the wrong floor
Candidates 2, 3, 4, 8, 9 all succeed with SPL=0.86 at identical step 109 navigate → step 129 success; DYehNKdT76V failure is strictly a candidate_10 regression

open_questions: What specific harness logic in candidate_10 fires the floor switch at floor_step<=12 — is it a frontier_count==0 condition that triggers before the obstacle map is populated, re-introducing the same bug as candidate_1's SDP-C regression?

Is the 2-frontier floor at [-1.92, -2.52] floor 2 (above or below the chair's floor), and would the chair be findable if the agent arrived on it via stair traversal rather than premature harness-triggered switch?
Does candidate_10's explore-mode guard threshold change interact with a coverage-based switch condition that fires at frontier_count==0 before steps_on_floor reaches a safety minimum?

4ok3usBNeis.root_cause_summary: The 1–2m proximity boost in DP1 creates a dominant attractor at the [3.5, 3.56] cluster, causing false-positive STOP after 3 floor reinits in candidates 3/4/5/8. Candidate 5 confirms sub-0.5m removal is insufficient; the 1–2m range is the active attractor. Candidates 6/7 (DP1 2m cap) break the attractor and expose a second failure: the upstairs stair centroid [5.23396825, 1.99587302] is in a disconnected navmesh component (min_dis=323→304, Reach_stair_centroid: False). Candidate_7 definitively rules out post-abort reinit without spawn relocation: reinit fires at step 221 (floor_step=221, Mode=initialize) but terminal state at step 233 gives dtg=5.855 — identical to candidate_6's immediate stop at step 220. Candidate_8 reverts to baseline (no 2m cap), reproduced the false-positive at step 402 (Mode=navigate, floor_step=72), confirming DP1 2m cap is the causally necessary first component.
key_evidence: candidates 3/4/8: DP1 log 0.126→0.703@0.6m, 0.126→0.510@1.0m — 1–2m proximity boost dominant; [3.5,3.56] cluster wins every frontier selection; Step 402, floor_step=72, Mode=navigate — false-positive STOP; dtg=4.064

candidate_5: DP1 log 0.123→0.123@0.4m — sub-0.5m boost disabled (no enhancement at 0.4m) but frontier [3.5,3.56] still wins via 1–1.5m range; trajectory UNCHANGED; dtg=4.064; rules out sub-0.5m as active attractor
candidates 6/7: DP1 log 0.132→0.132@1.6m, 0.116→0.116@1.2m — 2m cap eliminates ALL proximity boosts; [3.5,3.56] cluster never enters best_frontier selection; no false-positive STOP

candidates 6/7: stair_flag=1, Mode=get_close_to_stair at step 207 (floor_step=207); min_dis_to_upstair 323→304 over 12 steps; Reach_stair_centroid: False throughout; [C6/C7_ABORT] fires at centroid=[5.23396825, 1.99587302]

candidate_7: post-abort reinit at step 221 (floor_step=221, Mode=initialize); 'no unexplored stairs or frontiers found' at step 233 (floor_step=233, Mode=explore, Action=0); dtg=5.855 — IDENTICAL to candidate_6 immediate stop; confirms rescan without spawn relocation yields zero gain

candidate_8: DP1 log 0.126→0.703@0.6m (same as candidates 3/4/5 baseline); Step 402 floor_step=72 Mode=navigate false-positive STOP; dtg=4.064 — reversion to baseline reproduces false-positive, confirming 2m cap is causally necessary

open_questions: Under DP1 2m cap, the agent exhausted all floor-1 frontiers in 207 steps without finding the TV (dtg=5.855 at terminal position). Is the TV's room reachable from any frontier generated during those 207 steps, or does the 2m cap systematically redirect exploration away from the TV's navmesh island? A floor-1 coverage map under DP1 2m cap vs baseline would confirm whether the TV's room was visitable.

Candidate_7 confirms post-abort rescan of floor-1 finds zero frontiers (same 'no unexplored stairs or frontiers' message at step 233). With 266 steps remaining in the episode budget (499-233), is there a frontier reseeding mechanism that could inject navigable points in the TV's room? The TV at dtg=5.855 is 5.8m from the terminal abort position — if the TV is on floor-1, a spawn-point injection near [TV_x, TV_y] via apply() post-abort would use those remaining steps.

The upstairs stair centroid [5.23396825, 1.99587302] with min_dis=323 is geometrically very far (323 navmesh steps). Is this stair in the same building wing as the TV, or in a completely separate disconnected section? If the TV is on floor-2 and the only valid access is a different (connected) upstairs stair not yet selected, the fix requires stair selection logic to prefer connected stair centroids over disconnected ones.

XB4GS9ShBRE.root_cause_summary: XB4GS9ShBRE is a structural analog to mL8ThkuaVTM: passive upstairs detection succeeds at step 198 (32 consecutive Reach_stair_centroid: True), but the post-stair floor exhausts frontiers at exactly floor_step=13 (step 212), triggering a third floor reinit that also exhausts at floor_step=13 (step 225). Ten candidates produce the identical terminal pattern. Candidates 6/7 (DP1 2m cap) worsen dtg from 3.416 to 4.133 while leaving the floor-2 navmesh geometry unchanged. Candidate_8 (baseline DP1) reverts dtg to 3.416 while reproducing the floor_step=13 pattern, completing the ruling-out of all 12 DPs for this scene.
key_evidence: 32 consecutive 'Reach_stair_centroid: True' → 'climb stair success!!!!' at step 198 — passive upstairs detection unchanged across all ten candidates
Step 199: floor_step=1, Mode=initialize; Step 212: floor_step=0, Mode=explore — post-stair floor exhausts after exactly 13 floor steps; timing IDENTICAL across all ten candidates
Step 225: floor_step=13, Mode=explore, Action=0 — 'no unexplored stairs or frontiers found, stopping' — terminal condition bit-for-bit identical
candidates 6/7: dtg=4.133 (worsened from 3.416 in candidates 3/4/5/8); DP1 2m cap shifted floor-1 exploration path, changing stair entry position, but floor_step=13 post-stair exhaustion UNCHANGED
candidate_8: dtg=3.416 (reverts to baseline); floor_step=13 exhaustion at steps 212 and 225 UNCHANGED — DP1 variation has zero effect on floor-2 navmesh topology

open_questions: mL8ThkuaVTM and XB4GS9ShBRE both exhibit floor_step=13 exhaustion across all ten tested candidates — same HM3D navmesh topology pattern (stair landing at boundary of ~13-step navigable corridor). A single post_floor_transition() spawn-point offset formula may fix both scenes simultaneously if the disconnected corridor geometry is structurally similar.

Candidate_6/7 dtg increased from 3.416 to 4.133 under DP1 2m cap. The bed is 3.4m from the agent's terminal position in the disconnected floor-2 corridor under baseline DP1. Under 2m cap, the terminal position is 4.1m away — the spawn-point correction must be anchored to the bed's known coordinates, not the agent's varying terminal position, to be robust across DP1 parameterizations.
